howItWorks.intro: This resin pour volume calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Volume
rules.explanation: Cubic inches to fluid ounces conversion.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much resin do I need?
faq.answer: Calculate mold volume in cubic inches, multiply by 0.554 to get fluid ounces.

faq.question: Should I mix extra?
faq.answer: Yes, mix 10% extra to account for mixing cup residue and bubbles.

faq.question: Is the Resin Pour Volume Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard crafting and DIY formulas trusted by crafters and makers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What mold length should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate mold length value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Resin Pour Volume Calculator?
sections.body: The Resin Pour Volume Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your mold length, mold width, pour depth, you get instant results including total mixed volume, resin needed, hardener needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total mixed volume right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your mold length and need to find the right total mixed volume. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Mold Length (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your mold length — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: mold width, pour depth, mix ratio. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total mixed volume, resin needed, hardener needed. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your mold length before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total mixed volume and resin needed — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your projects.
If you're unsure about your mix ratio, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
